title: Chapter 12 Quiz: Object-Oriented PHP
description: Test your knowledge of OOP in PHP!

questions.question: What keyword is used to define a class?
questions.explanation: The `class` keyword defines a class.

questions.question: An object is an instance of a class.
questions.explanation: Yes, objects are created from classes.

questions.question: How do you access a property `$color` of an object `$car`?
questions.explanation: Use the object operator `->` to access properties.

questions.question: Inside a class method, how do you refer to the current object?
questions.explanation: $this refers to the current object instance.

questions.question: A constructor method is named `__construct()`.
questions.explanation: Yes, the constructor is a magic method with that name.

questions.question: Which visibility modifier makes a property accessible only within the same class?
questions.explanation: private is accessible only within the defining class.

questions.question: What keyword is used to inherit a class?
questions.explanation: extends is used for class inheritance.

questions.question: A class can extend multiple classes in PHP.
questions.explanation: PHP supports single inheritance only; a class can extend only one parent.

questions.question: Which keyword allows a child class to call a parent's method?
questions.explanation: parent:: is used to call the parent's version of a method.

questions.question: What is the output of `Child::test()` if `test()` uses `self::who()` and `Child` overrides `who()`? (assuming code)
questions.explanation: self:: binds to the class where the method is defined (the parent), so Parent::who() is called.

questions.question: Static methods can be called without creating an object.
questions.explanation: Yes, using ClassName::method().

questions.question: Which of the following is used to define an interface?
questions.explanation: The `interface` keyword defines an interface.

questions.question: A class can implement multiple interfaces. True or false?
questions.explanation: Yes, a class can implement multiple interfaces (separated by commas).

questions.question: What is an abstract class?
options: A class that cannot be instantiated
A class with only abstract methods
A class that is declared final
A class that cannot be inherited
questions.explanation: An abstract class cannot be instantiated; it may contain abstract methods.

questions.question: Traits are used to achieve multiple inheritance in PHP.
questions.explanation: Traits allow code reuse across unrelated classes, providing a form of multiple inheritance.

questions.question: Which magic method is called when an object is treated as a string?
questions.explanation: __toString() is called when an object is used in a string context.

questions.question: What does the `final` keyword do when applied to a class?
options: Prevents the class from being instantiated
Prevents the class from being inherited
questions.explanation: A final class cannot be extended.

questions.question: A protected property can be accessed from outside the class.
questions.explanation: Protected members are accessible only within the class and its descendants.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about an interface method?
questions.explanation: Interface methods are implicitly public and have no body.

questions.question: What does the `::class` constant return?
options: The class name as a string
questions.explanation: ClassName::class returns the fully qualified class name as a string.

questions.question: Late static binding uses the `static::` keyword.
questions.explanation: Yes, static:: resolves at runtime based on the called class.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to import a trait inside a class?
questions.explanation: Inside a class, you write `use TraitName;`.

questions.question: What will `new self()` do inside a class method?
options: Create an instance of the current class
Create an instance of the parent class
Create an instance of the called class
questions.explanation: self refers to the class where the code is written, so it creates an instance of that class.

questions.question: A constructor can return a value.
questions.explanation: Constructors cannot return a value; they are for initialization only.

questions.question: Which magic method is called when an object is used as a function?
questions.explanation: __invoke() is triggered when an object is called as a function.
